summary: Comprehensive testing of TrustOffice Disposition/Sale of Asset flow. All backend API tests passed (19/19). UI testing confirmed: Schedule A page with Active/All tabs, status badges, dispose modal with create minutes option, and disposition minutes template with URL params pre-fill.

issues: Console warning: 'In HTML, <tr> cannot be a child of <span>' - likely from Visual Editor wrapper, not affecting functionality

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/schedule-a/{item_id}/dispose marks asset as disposed
Backend: Dispose endpoint with minimal required fields only
Backend: Already disposed asset returns 400 error
Backend: All disposition reason types work (sale, transfer, donation, destruction)
Backend: Disposed asset hidden in active view, visible in all view
Backend: Schedule A summary returns correct totals
Backend: Disposition template marks asset as disposed with minutes reference
UI: Schedule A page loads with Active/All tabs
UI: Summary cards show Active Assets count and Disposed count
UI: Dispose button opens modal for active assets
UI: Dispose modal shows asset info and all fields
UI: Create minutes checkbox toggles button text
UI: Continue to Minutes navigates to disposition template with URL params
UI: Disposition template pre-fills asset data from URL params
critical_code_review_comments: Dispose endpoint correctly validates already-disposed assets (400 error)
URL params correctly pass asset_id, description, value from Schedule A to disposition template
Both dispose paths work: direct dispose and continue-to-minutes
Status filtering properly hides disposed assets from active view
MOCKED: Postmark email service (sandbox mode) - no email actually sent
seed_data_creation: Test assets created with 'TEST' prefix and cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Disposition flow fully tested. Two paths verified: 1) Direct dispose via POST /api/schedule-a/{item_id}/dispose endpoint marks asset as disposed immediately. 2) Continue to minutes path navigates to /minutes/template/disposition_of_asset with URL params (asset_id, description, value, etc) which pre-fills the disposition template form. Schedule A page shows Active/All tabs with status badges. Disposed assets hidden from active view.

disposition_template.schedule_a_asset_selector: Loads active assets from Schedule A
